source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that a unique Certificate Authority is used for etcd

input_row.source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that a unique Certificate Authority is used for etcd
input_row.description: Kubernetes	can	audit	the	details	of	requests	made	to	the	API	server.	The	--audit-policy- file	flag	must	be	set	for	this	logging	to	be	enabled.
input_row.rationale: Logging	is	an	important	detective	control	for	all	systems,	to	detect	potential	unauthorised access. Impact: Audit	logs	will	be	created	on	the	master	nodes,	which	will	consume	disk	space.	Care	should be	taken	to	avoid	generating	too	large	volumes	of	log	information	as	this	could	impact	the available	of	the	cluster	nodes.
input_row.audit: Run	the	following	command	on	one	of	the	cluster	master	nodes: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify	that	the	--audit-policy-file	is	set.	Review	the	contents	of	the	file	specified	and ensure	that	it	contains	a	valid	audit	policy.
input_row.remediation: Create	an	audit	policy	file	for	your	cluster. Default	Value: Unless	the	--audit-policy-file	flag	is	specified,	no	auditing	will	be	carried	out. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug-application-cluster/audit/ 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/91406.htm 156	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	7 6.2	Activate	audit	logging Ensure	that	local	logging	has	been	enabled	on	all	systems	and	networking	devices. 157	|	P a g e
input_row.impact: Audit	logs	will	be	created	on	the	master	nodes,	which	will	consume	disk	space.	Care	should be	taken	to	avoid	generating	too	large	volumes	of	log	information	as	this	could	impact	the available	of	the	cluster	nodes.
input_row.references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/debug-application-cluster/audit/ 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/91406.htm 156	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	7 6.2	Activate	audit	logging Ensure	that	local	logging	has	been	enabled	on	all	systems	and	networking	devices. 157	|	P a g e

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- The audit process described involves running a command on one of the cluster master nodes to check for the presence of a specific file configuration (`--audit-policy-file`). This requires accessing the EC2 instances hosting the Kubernetes master nodes directly, which indicates a manual approach.
- The audit requires checking a file's contents manually to ensure a valid audit policy, which cannot be accomplished via Alibaba Cloud APIs or Aliyun CLI/Alibaba Cloud CLI.
- The references note that this control cannot be audited or modified in ACK managed clusters through automated means.
- The audit involves evaluating the system configuration and setup at the node level, which typically requires SSH access and is not exposed via standard API calls.

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. **SSH Access to Master Node:**
   - Use the Alibaba Cloud Console to obtain SSH credentials for accessing the Kubernetes master nodes in your ACK cluster.
   - Navigate to the Kubernetes master node where you want to verify the setup.

2. **Execute Command on Master Node:**
   - Run the command `ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver` to identify running instances of the kube-apiserver process.

3. **Verify Presence of Audit Policy File:**
   - Check the output for the presence of `--audit-policy-file` in the list of start-up flags for the kube-apiserver process.

4. **Review Audit Policy File:**
   - Locate the path specified for `--audit-policy-file` in the kube-apiserver command line output.
   - Inspect the contents of this file to ensure it contains a valid audit policy configuration following Kubernetes documentation standards.

5. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - Verify that the audit policy file explicitly defines audit rules that conform to security and logging best practices.
   - Ensure the policy aligns with organizational security requirements and retention periods as applicable.

By following these steps, you can ensure that the system's configuration is up to standard and that audit logging is correctly set up to capture necessary events. This manual verification helps maintain compliance with the outlined CIS recommendations.
